If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. A sample test file (`Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs`) and a standalone sample markdown (`samples/Sample_Toolboxes_CRUD.md`) were added. The README was updated with a Toolboxes section, but it is incomplete and has pre-existing errors that need correction.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Wrong package name in \\u0026#34;Install the package\\u0026#34; section**: The README shows `dotnet add package Azure.AI.Extensions.OpenAI --prerelease` — this is the wrong package. It must be `dotnet add package Azure.AI.Projects.Agents --prerelease`.\\n- **Incorrect boilerplate in \\u0026#34;Install the package\\u0026#34; section**: The note below the install command mentions \\u0026#34;Cosmos DB account (SQL API)\\u0026#34; and \\u0026#34;C# 8.0 syntax\\u0026#34; — this is leftover boilerplate from a different package template and is irrelevant to Azure AI Agents.\\n- **Table of Contents / section heading mismatch**: The Table of Contents lists \\u0026#34;Prompt Agents\\u0026#34; but the actual heading in the Examples section is \\u0026#34;## Declarative Agents\\u0026#34;. The ToC anchor `#prompt-agents` will not resolve.\\n- **Incomplete Toolboxes section in README**: The `### Toolboxes` subsection only demonstrates creating, getting a ToolboxRecord, and getting a ToolboxVersion. It is missing snippet examples for: updating the default version (`UpdateToolbox`), listing all versions (`GetToolboxVersions`), listing all toolboxes (`GetToolboxes`), deleting a specific version (`DeleteToolboxVersion`), and deleting all versions (`DeleteToolbox`). The sample `.cs` file already has `#region Snippet:` blocks for all of these.\\n- **No Key Concepts entry for `AgentToolboxes`**: The `## Key concepts` section has no explanation of what Toolboxes are, that `AgentToolboxes` is obtained via `agentsClient.GetAgentToolboxes()`, or that this feature requires the `Toolboxes=V1Preview` experimental header.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md` — **Edit**\\n\\n**Fix 1 — Wrong package name and boilerplate.** Replace the entire \\u0026#34;Install the package\\u0026#34; subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Install the package\\n\\nInstall the client library for .NET with [NuGet]((www.nuget.org/redacted)\\n\\n```\\ndotnet add package Azure.AI.Projects.Agents --prerelease\\n```\\n```\\n\\nRemove the incorrect note that follows about \\u0026#34;Azure subscription and Cosmos DB account (SQL API)\\u0026#34; and \\u0026#34;C# 8.0 syntax\\u0026#34;.\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Fix 2 — Table of Contents anchor mismatch.** In the Table of Contents, change:\\n\\n```markdown\\n  - [Prompt Agents](#prompt-agents)\\n```\\n\\nto:\\n\\n```markdown\\n  - [Declarative Agents](#declarative-agents)\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Fix 3 — Key Concepts: add Toolboxes concept.** In the `## Key concepts` section (after the existing `### Service API versions` subsection), add a new subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Agent Toolboxes\\n\\n`AgentToolboxes` is a sub-client used to store and manage collections of tools in Azure so that Agents can retrieve and use them. A **toolbox** may have multiple versions; each version holds a list of `ProjectsAgentTool` objects. A `ToolboxRecord` represents a toolbox by name and tracks its default version, while `ToolboxVersion` represents a specific version with its full tool list.\\n\\n`AgentToolboxes` is obtained from `AgentAdministrationClient`:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** This feature requires the `Toolboxes=V1Preview` experimental header and has the `[Experimental(\\u0026#34;AAIP001\\u0026#34;)]` attribute. Suppress the `AAIP001` diagnostic before using these APIs.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Fix 4 — Incomplete Toolboxes section.** In the `### Toolboxes` subsection under `## Examples`, extend the section to include the missing operations. After the existing snippets for GetToolbox and GetToolboxVersion, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\nTo update the default version of a toolbox, use `UpdateToolboxAsync`:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nTo list all versions of a toolbox, use `GetToolboxVersionsAsync`:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nTo list all toolboxes, use `GetToolboxesAsync`:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nTo delete toolboxes, use `DeleteToolboxVersionAsync` to remove a specific version, or `DeleteToolboxAsync` to remove all versions. Note that the current default version cannot be deleted directly; change the default version first.\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAll required `#region Snippet:` regions are already present in the existing sample test file:\\n`sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs`\\n\\nThe following snippet names exist in that file and need corresponding placeholder fences added to README.md (as described in Step 1, Fix 4 above):\\n\\n- `Sample_UpdateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async`\\n- `Sample_ListToolboxVersions_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async`\\n- `Sample_ListToolboxes_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async`\\n- `Sample_DeleteToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async`\\n\\nFor the Key Concepts snippet (Fix 3):\\n- `Sample_CreateClient_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD` — already has a `#region` block in the test file; the `#if SNIPPET` guard hides the test-specific lines correctly.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md` currently contains all required top-level sections. No sections are missing, but the following need correction:\\n\\n1. **Getting started \\u0026gt; Install the package** — wrong package name and irrelevant boilerplate (described in Fix 1)\\n2. **Table of contents** — anchor mismatch for \\u0026#34;Prompt Agents\\u0026#34; vs \\u0026#34;Declarative Agents\\u0026#34; (described in Fix 2)\\n3. **Key concepts** — missing `AgentToolboxes` concept entry (described in Fix 3)\\n4. **Examples \\u0026gt; Toolboxes** — incomplete; missing update, list, and delete snippet examples (described in Fix 4)\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. The ToC anchor `#prompt-agents` will not resolve.\\n- **Incomplete Toolboxes section in README**: The `### Toolboxes` subsection only demonstrates creating, getting a ToolboxRecord, and getting a ToolboxVersion. It is missing snippet examples for: updating the default version (`UpdateToolbox`), listing all versions (`GetToolboxVersions`), listing all toolboxes (`GetToolboxes`), deleting a specific version (`DeleteToolboxVersion`), and deleting all versions (`DeleteToolbox`). The sample `.cs` file already has `#region Snippet:` blocks for all of these.\\n- **No Key Concepts entry for `AgentToolboxes`**: The `## Key concepts` section has no explanation of what Toolboxes are, that `AgentToolboxes` is obtained via `agentsClient.GetAgentToolboxes()`, or that this feature requires the `Toolboxes=V1Preview` experimental header.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md` — **Edit**\\n\\n**Fix 1 — Wrong package name and boilerplate.** Replace the entire \\u0026#34;Install the package\\u0026#34; subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Install the package\\n\\nInstall the client library for .NET with [NuGet]((www.nuget.org/redacted)\\n\\n```\\ndotnet add package Azure.AI.Projects.Agents --prerelease\\n```\\n```\\n\\nRemove the incorrect note that follows about \\u0026#34;Azure subscription and Cosmos DB account (SQL API)\\u0026#34; and \\u0026#34;C# 8.0 syntax\\u0026#34;.\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Fix 2 — Table of Contents anchor mismatch.** In the Table of Contents, change:\\n\\n```markdown\\n  - [Prompt Agents](#prompt-agents)\\n```\\n\\nto:\\n\\n```markdown\\n  - [Declarative Agents](#declarative-agents)\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Fix 3 — Key Concepts: add Toolboxes concept.** In the `## Key concepts` section (after the existing `### Service API versions` subsection), add a new subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Agent Toolboxes\\n\\n`AgentToolboxes` is a sub-client used to store and manage collections of tools in Azure so that Agents can retrieve and use them. A **toolbox** may have multiple versions; each version holds a list of `ProjectsAgentTool` objects. A `ToolboxRecord` represents a toolbox by name and tracks its default version, while `ToolboxVersion` represents a specific version with its full tool list.\\n\\n`AgentToolboxes` is obtained from `AgentAdministrationClient`:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** This feature requires the `Toolboxes=V1Preview` experimental header and has the `[Experimental(\\u0026#34;AAIP001\\u0026#34;)]` attribute. Suppress the `AAIP001` diagnostic before using these APIs.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Fix 4 — Incomplete Toolboxes section.** In the `### Toolboxes` subsection under `## Examples`, extend the section to include the missing operations. After the existing snippets for GetToolbox and GetToolboxVersion, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\nTo update the default version of a toolbox, use `UpdateToolboxAsync`:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nTo list all versions of a toolbox, use `GetToolboxVersionsAsync`:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nTo list all toolboxes, use `GetToolboxesAsync`:\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nTo delete toolboxes, use `DeleteToolboxVersionAsync` to remove a specific version, or `DeleteToolboxAsync` to remove all versions. Note that the current default version cannot be deleted directly; change the default version first.\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAll required `#region Snippet:` regions are already present in the existing sample test file:\\n`sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/tests/Samples/Sample_TooboxesCRUD.cs`\\n\\nThe following snippet names exist in that file and need corresponding placeholder fences added to README.md (as described in Step 1, Fix 4 above):\\n\\n- `Sample_UpdateToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async`\\n- `Sample_ListToolboxVersions_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async`\\n- `Sample_ListToolboxes_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async`\\n- `Sample_DeleteToolbox_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD_Async`\\n\\nFor the Key Concepts snippet (Fix 3):\\n- `Sample_CreateClient_ToolboxesAgentsCRUD` — already has a `#region` block in the test file; the `#if SNIPPET` guard hides the test-specific lines correctly.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/README.md` currently contains all required top-level sections. No sections are missing, but the following need correction:\\n\\n1. **Getting started \\u0026gt; Install the package** — wrong package name and irrelevant boilerplate (described in Fix 1)\\n2. **Table of contents** — anchor mismatch for \\u0026#34;Prompt Agents\\u0026#34; vs \\u0026#34;Declarative Agents\\u0026#34; (described in Fix 2)\\n3. **Key concepts** — missing `AgentToolboxes` concept entry (described in Fix 3)\\n4. **Examples \\u0026gt; Toolboxes** — incomplete; missing update, list, and delete snippet examples (described in Fix 4)\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next.\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 ai` — check the git diff after running to confirm README was updated correctly with injected snippet content\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 ai` — no public API changes are expected for this documentation-only fix; run to confirm\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Projects.Agents/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24111720256/agentic_workflow) · ● 447.4K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57908\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-08T00:55:45Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-08T00:55:45Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
